title: WIP - revert to storage v1.12.13
body:
The latest image has caused some flakes in the past two days. It is
unclear what has caused the issue, likely an outdated mirror. Using
a stable image should the update rate and thereby the chance of causing
flakes.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Unfortunately the problem appears to be more systemic, and just changing image did not resolve it.

> Unfortunately the problem appears to be more systemic, and just changing image did not resolve it.

It only targets the `save ... different digest` error in the e2e tests.
Which I just hit again locally...

It happens with `:30` as well now :-/ I'll chase that down.
For the record:
```
[libpod (change-fm)]$ ./bin/podman save -o fm.tar registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal:30
Error: unable to save "registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal:30": Error copying image to the remote destination: Error writing blob: Digest did not match, expected sha256:f4119231eb4d87dfc14c23d0f0bbf7cdb88946d21a2c44374923b79dec7638ef, got sha256:8ae3b674494bd7b6d62b8ff72071a6a4d1b2294ea254919bc84b8742f9a30667
```
I can't reproduce with v1.4.4.
@nalind, I don't get the error with storage v1.12.13 but with storage v1.12.6.
The regression starts with storage v1.12.14. I am going to roll back to v1.12.13 to unblock CI.
@mheon I pushed a commit rolling back to v1.12.13 to this PR so we preserve the discussion.
I bisected and found that https://github.com/containers/storage/commit/0d4a46ce285b0f5295561e1845f75537e80d2d53 introduces the regression.
@nalind, it looks like the tarlogger has side effects. Using a dummy logger for the compressed stream makes the `save` pass but the `load` on the archive then fails with a digest mismatch.
Can we get this fixed upstream, potentially? I kind of want to have a newer c/storage with the ignore chown errors option available for 1.5
@mheon, I am currently looking into it but this thing is really hairy.
Ack. We can release without if things don't look promising.
I had another look and found that using `os.Pipe()` alters the digest.  Using `io.Pipe()` requires a little workaround as it doesn't accept writes after being closed. I'll wrap up a PR for storage...

I now vendored my fork of c/storage to test https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/406. It works locally on my machine. To be clear: I have no clue why using `os.Pipe()` has the side-effect but I hope to find that out at some point.
